batch_summary: As Rachel prepares to move in with Ross, she and Monica sort through her belongings, finding she only owns a magnetic bottle opener. Ross awkwardly flirts with Rachel, prompting Phoebe to confront him about his secret marriage to Rachel and his hopes that she will fall in love with him. Ross deflects the accusation by claiming Phoebe is the one in love with Rachel. After a brief dispute between Monica and Rachel over candlesticks, Ross receives a call offering him a position at NYU's paleontology department.
cumulative_summary: In the apartment, Rachel prepares to move in with Ross, but she and Monica discover that her only kitchen contribution is a magnetic bottle opener. Seizing the moment, Ross awkwardly flirts with Rachel, claiming she completes him before quickly backtracking. Phoebe intervenes, calling Ross out for his hidden agenda: he secretly hopes Rachel will fall in love with him, despite the fact that he has not yet confessed they are already married. To deflect, Ross jokingly accuses Phoebe of being obsessed and in love with Rachel herself. Meanwhile, tension simmers between Monica and Rachel as they bicker over who owns a pair of candlesticks. Ross is saved from further interrogation when he receives a phone call from the head of the paleontology department at NYU, securing a new opportunity starting Monday, which he proudly shares with the group.

batch_summary: Ross excitedly shares that NYU has invited him to be a guest lecturer, which could lead to a full-time professorship. Phoebe nearly exposes Ross's secret marriage to Rachel but covers it up as a joke at the last second, warning Ross she "saved his ass." Later, at Central Perk, Phoebe casually informs the shocked group that her psychic predicted she will die this week, leaving everyone skeptical and concerned.

While Phoebe is bummed by the "worst reading" possible, her friends react with deep skepticism and concern.

batch_summary: Phoebe decides to live her final week to the fullest following her psychic's prediction. Meanwhile, Ross arrives at Central Perk to practice his first NYU guest lecture on the group. His presentation on sediment flow rate is incredibly dry and monotone, prompting relentless teasing from his friends. Joey mocks his intense focus on his note cards, Chandler suggests opening with a joke, and Phoebe quips that the boredom will kill her. Defensive and discouraged, Ross prepares to leave, but Rachel attempts to offer advice to make it less boring.

batch_summary: Frustrated by Joey's inappropriate suggestions for visual aids and Chandler's teasing, Ross decides to prepare his NYU lecture on his own. Meanwhile, Chandler delivers mail to Joey revealing that his health insurance has expired because he did not work enough over the past year. Anxious about the financial risk of getting hurt without coverage, Joey realizes he must be careful and immediately heads out to see his agent to find a new acting job.

batch_summary: Joey visits his agent, Estelle, to find work after losing his health insurance.
